problemTitle: Count the Number of Complete Components

sources.code:
# Time:  O(n)
# Space: O(n)

# bfs
class Solution(object):
    def countCompleteComponents(self, n, edges):
        """
        :type n: int
        :type edges: List[List[int]]
        :rtype: int
        """
        def bfs(u):
            if lookup[u]:
                return False
            v_cnt = e_cnt = 0
            lookup[u] = True
            q = [u]
            while q:
                new_q = []
                v_cnt += len(q)
                for u in q:
                    e_cnt += len(adj[u])
                    for v in adj[u]:
                        if lookup[v]:
                            continue
                        lookup[v] = True
                        new_q.append(v)
                q = new_q
            return v_cnt*(v_cnt-1) == e_cnt

        adj = [[] for _ in xrange(n)]
        for u, v in edges:
            adj[u].append(v)
            adj[v].append(u)
        lookup = [False]*n
        return sum(bfs(u) for u in xrange(n) if not lookup[u])

# Time:  O(n)
# Space: O(n)

# bfs
class Solution(object):
    def countCompleteComponents(self, n, edges):
        """
        :type n: int
        :type edges: List[List[int]]
        :rtype: int
        """
        def bfs(u):
            if lookup[u]:
                return False
            v_cnt = e_cnt = 0
            lookup[u] = True
            q = [u]
            while q:
                new_q = []
                v_cnt += len(q)
                for u in q:
                    e_cnt += len(adj[u])
                    for v in adj[u]:
                        if lookup[v]:
                            continue
                        lookup[v] = True
                        new_q.append(v)
                q = new_q
            return v_cnt*(v_cnt-1) == e_cnt

        adj = [[] for _ in xrange(n)]
        for u, v in edges:
            adj[u].append(v)
            adj[v].append(u)
        lookup = [False]*n
        return sum(bfs(u) for u in xrange(n) if not lookup[u])
sources.reviewedCode:
# Time:  O(n)
# Space: O(n)

# bfs
# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Traversal tells you which nodes belong together; then you ask whether that set contains every internal edge it should.
class Solution(object):
    def countCompleteComponents(self, n, edges):
        """
        :type n: int
        :type edges: List[List[int]]
        :rtype: int
        """
        def bfs(u):
            if lookup[u]:
                return False
            # [Reader note] State setup
            # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Component extraction, structural validation, and translating a graph property into a precise per-component check.
            v_cnt = e_cnt = 0
            lookup[u] = True
            q = [u]
            # [Reader note] Main pass
            # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
            while q:
                new_q = []
                v_cnt += len(q)
                for u in q:
                    e_cnt += len(adj[u])
                    for v in adj[u]:
                        # [Reader note] Decision point
                        # Pay attention here: The trick is not fancy traversal; it is making the completeness test exact instead of relying on vague visual intuition.
                        if lookup[v]:
                            continue
                        lookup[v] = True
                        new_q.append(v)
                q = new_q
            return v_cnt*(v_cnt-1) == e_cnt

        adj = [[] for _ in xrange(n)]
        for u, v in edges:
            adj[u].append(v)
            adj[v].append(u)
        lookup = [False]*n
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return sum(bfs(u) for u in xrange(n) if not lookup[u])

sources.reviewedCode:
# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Traversal tells you which nodes belong together; then you ask whether that set contains every internal edge it should.
class Solution:
    def countCompleteComponents(self, n: int, edges: List[List[int]]) -> int:
        def dfs(i: int) -> (int, int):
            vis[i] = True
            # [Reader note] State setup
            # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Component extraction, structural validation, and translating a graph property into a precise per-component check.
            x, y = 1, len(g[i])
            # [Reader note] Main pass
            # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
            for j in g[i]:
                # [Reader note] Decision point
                # Pay attention here: The trick is not fancy traversal; it is making the completeness test exact instead of relying on vague visual intuition.
                if not vis[j]:
                    a, b = dfs(j)
                    x += a
                    y += b
            return x, y

        g = defaultdict(list)
        for a, b in edges:
            g[a].append(b)
            g[b].append(a)
        vis = [False] * n
        ans = 0
        for i in range(n):
            if not vis[i]:
                a, b = dfs(i)
                ans += a * (a - 1) == b
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return ans
